sources.evidence: The supplied Linktree identifies @ashdaviesmusicanddrums as a Melbourne composer and drummer and links directly to Ashley Davies’ official music website, Bandcamp, Facebook, Instagram and YouTube properties.
The official site identifies Ashley Davies, publishes his biography and work, and cross-links the same Bandcamp, Facebook, Instagram and YouTube destinations as the supplied Linktree.
Both the supplied Linktree and official website link to Ashley Davies’ Bandcamp catalogue; the official site directs fans there to listen to and buy his releases.
Exact Bandcamp destination is cross-linked by the supplied artist Linktree and Ashley Davies’ official website.
Exact Facebook destination is published on the supplied artist Linktree and cross-linked from the official Ashley Davies website.
Exact Instagram destination is published on the supplied artist Linktree and cross-linked from the official Ashley Davies website.
Ashley Davies’ official website links to this Music and Drumming channel; the channel links back to ashleydavies.com.au and describes his six solo instrumental albums and festival work.
The official channel’s Popular view showed Ned Kelly by Ashley Davies & Ian Jones at 3.9K views, ahead of V.F.T. at 1.7K and Mambo Wah Wah at 1.3K.
The 2025 Curb interview speaks directly with Ashley Davies about GOLD, his uncle Len Davies and the Lasseter’s Reef project.
Neither the supplied Linktree nor official artist website provides a Spotify profile; the button is intentionally omitted.
The official shop is presented as an album store rather than a general merchandise shop; Buy Music and Bandcamp cover the verified purchase destinations without mislabelling.
